title: The Old Growth
desc: Maxxor walked into a trap for the memory of a friend. You might be the only one who can get him out.
chainDesc: A conversation about an old friend draws you into Maxxor's deepest wound.

steps.text:
The Forest of Life. The deeper you go, the thicker the canopy gets, until sunlight is just a memory filtering through layers of ancient green. Your scanner range drops — Gadrel was right, the old growth smothers signals like a blanket.

But you don't need long range. You need close range. And there — through the undergrowth — you can hear voices.

You creep forward and find a clearing. Maxxor is there, standing in the center. He looks smaller somehow. Not physically — he's still eight feet of OverWorld muscle. But the way he's holding himself. Shoulders down. Guard lowered. He's listening.

Across from him, a Danian. Not a soldier. An emissary, or at least dressed like one. He's speaking softly, and Maxxor is letting him.

Your scanner catches what your ears can't at this distance. You boost the gain and pick up fragments:

"...Raznus remembers, Maxxor. Not everything. But enough. He asked me to come. He asked me to tell you..."

Maxxor's hands are shaking. You've never seen that before.

Then your scanner picks up something else. Four bio-signatures. Flanking the clearing from the tree line. Masked — the same dampening tech you saw at Ravanaugh Ridge. They're moving into position. The emissary is still talking. Maxxor is still listening.

It's an ambush. And Maxxor doesn't see it.

options.label: Shout a warning — blow your cover but give Maxxor time
Use your scanner to pinpoint the assassins' exact positions and relay them to Maxxor

steps.text:
"MAXXOR! FOUR IN THE TREES!"

Your voice cracks through the clearing like a whip. The emissary flinches. Maxxor doesn't flinch — he ignites.

Fire erupts from his hands in the same instant the four Danians burst from the tree line. They're fast — purpose-built for this, armoured and carrying vials of that parasitic compound you scanned at the ridge. One swing and Maxxor would be infected, just like Raznus.

But they expected to hit him from behind while his guard was down. They didn't expect him to be ready.

Maxxor catches the first one mid-leap with a backhand that sends it through a tree trunk. The second gets a face full of fire. The third hesitates — and that's enough. Maxxor closes the distance and puts it down.

The fourth turns and runs. The emissary is already gone.

Maxxor stands in the burning clearing, chest heaving. He looks at the vials the assassins were carrying. He knows what's in them. He's seen what they do.

Then he looks at you. Standing at the edge of the clearing with your scanner and nothing else.

"You."

His voice is raw.

options.label: Show him your scanner data from Ravanaugh Ridge

steps.text:
Your scanner locks onto all four positions simultaneously and you transmit the data directly — a trick you learned calibrating in dense environments. Maxxor's eyes snap to each position in sequence, faster than you can track.

He moves like lightning.

The first Danian doesn't even clear the tree line before Maxxor is on it. The second drops from a branch and catches fire mid-air. By the time the third and fourth realize the ambush has failed, Maxxor has already closed the distance. It's over in seconds.

The emissary tries to run. Maxxor lets him. He's not interested in the bait anymore.

He stands in the clearing, surrounded by unconscious Danians and scattered vials of parasitic compound. He picks one up and stares at it. The same thing that took Raznus from him. They were going to use it again. On him.

Then he turns to you.

"You mapped them before I even knew they were there."

His voice is quiet.

options.label: Show him your scanner data from Ravanaugh Ridge

steps.text:
You show him everything. The three Danians at the ridge. The signal dampeners. The parasite samples. The patrol route maps. All of it.

Maxxor scrolls through the data. His expression doesn't change. When he's done, he hands your scanner back and picks up one of the parasite vials from the ground. Holds it up to the light filtering through the canopy. Studies it like he's reading something written very small.

Then he crushes it. Wipes his hand on his leg.

"The message said Raznus was asking for me." He says it simply. Like he's reporting a fact. "I knew what it probably was."

He doesn't elaborate. He doesn't need to. You both know he came anyway.

The clearing is still smoking. A bird lands on a branch overhead, decides it doesn't like the smell, and leaves again. Maxxor watches it go.

"Gadrel said you talked about him like he was a person. Not a cautionary tale."

He's quiet for a while. Not struggling with something — just letting it be quiet.

"Most creatures who help me want something. A position. A favour. Access." He glances at your scanner. "A scan. You went to the ridge because I asked. You came here because you figured out I was in trouble. And the first thing you did in Kiru City was help an old soldier pick up his groceries."

The corner of his mouth moves. Not quite a smile. Close.

He steps into the open and stands still. The ambient heat he always carries drops away. It's the most deliberate thing you've ever seen him do.

"Go ahead."
